title: Sun Pharma Clarifies No Operational Impact from Arrest of Independent Director Gautam Doshi
summary: Mumbai: Sun Pharmaceutical Industries Limited has informed stock exchanges about the arrest of its Independent Director, Gautam Doshi, while clarifying that the development is unrelated to the...
thbAction: Position THB's pharma CRM and omnichannel HCP engagement workflows to support launch planning, KAM orchestration, and compliant follow-up across India and the Middle East.
contentHtml:
Mumbai: Sun Pharmaceutical Industries Limited has informed stock exchanges about the arrest of its Independent Director, Gautam Doshi, while clarifying that the development is unrelated to the company's business operations and is not expected to affect its functioning or governance processes.

The disclosure was made by the pharmaceutical major under Regulation 30 of the Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI) Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements Regulations, 2015. In its regulatory filing, the company stated that it had received information regarding the arrest of Doshi and was notifying investors and stock exchanges in compliance with applicable disclosure requirements.

Sun Pharma emphasized that the matter does not pertain to the company, its operations, management, or business activities. The company further stated that it does not foresee any impact on its day-to-day operations, financial performance, or the functioning of its Board of Directors as a result of the development.

Gautam Doshi serves as an Independent Director on the board of Sun Pharmaceutical Industries Limited and is also associated with certain other companies in a non-executive capacity. As an Independent Director, he is not involved in the day-to-day management or executive operations of the company.

The company stated that it would continue to monitor the situation and assess any further developments, if necessary. Sun Pharma also indicated that appropriate steps would be taken depending on future developments relating to the matter.

Media reports cited in connection with the arrest have linked the matter to investigations unrelated to Sun Pharma's business activities. However, the company reiterated that the issue does not concern its operations and does not affect its governance framework or business continuity.

BusinessWorld reports that Sun Pharma clarified that the arrest of Gautam Doshi is unrelated to the company and that no impact is anticipated on its operations, management, or Board processes. The company made the disclosure as part of its regulatory obligations and assured stakeholders that it would keep monitoring the situation.
